methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
d-Matrix is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Sid Sheth, currently valued at $2.0B post-money after raising $450M. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip players, with 3 directly comparable same-sector, same-stage peers (Etched at $5.0B, Substrate at $1.0B, Rain AI at $500M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression anchors on the current $2.0B post-money and applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A advancement, implying $4.0B. Peer-Median derives valuation from the 3 comparable private companies, yielding a median of $1.0B against a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because d-Matrix's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the semiconductor sector's 24.2x median price-to-sales multiple. 

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $4.6B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: peer valuations in the frontier-chip space exhibit high variance, and stage-progression methods are sensitive to assumption of step-up multipliers. Current valuation of $2.0B sits below the median estimate, suggesting modest upside but significant downside risk depending on capital efficiency and technology differentiation relative to the peer set.
